key_risk_factors: No region-specific data residency guarantees; data may be stored in any multi‑tenant location
Customer data is used for product improvement by default, requiring manual opt‑out
Only SOC 2 Type I certification (no Type II audit) limiting assurance of ongoing security controls
Vendor maturity is low (18 months, 12 enterprise customers) increasing operational and support risk
missing_information: Specific encryption algorithms, key management processes, and rotation schedules
Detailed API rate‑limit thresholds, throttling behavior, and error handling
Comprehensive data retention, deletion, and backup policies, especially for regulated documents
Incident response plan, breach notification timelines, and liability clauses
business_recommendation: Engage the vendor only after negotiating contractual safeguards: enforce data residency options, require opt‑in consent for data usage, obtain SOC 2 Type II audit evidence, clarify SLA uptime and penalties, and secure detailed API and security documentation.
